task: How do vector embeddings and vector databases work together to enable semantic search in a RAG system?

steps.input: How do vector embeddings and vector databases work together to enable semantic search in a RAG system?

tool_result.query: How do vector embeddings and vector databases work together to enable semantic search in a RAG system?

results.title: Vector Databases and Embeddings
results.snippet: Vector databases are specialized storage systems optimized for efficient similarity search over high-dimensional vector embeddings. They are foundational for RAG systems, semantic search, recommendation systems, and anomaly detection. results.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
results.snippet: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) enhances language model responses by grounding them in relevant external knowledge retrieved at inference time. Instead of relying solely on parameters, RAG dynamically retrieves relevant documents and includes them as context. The RAG pipeline has two phases: indexing and retrieval-generation. results.title: NLP Evaluation Metrics
results.snippet: Evaluating NLP models requires metrics aligned with task objectives. Different tasks need different metrics, and no single metric captures all aspects of quality. For text generation: BLEU measures n-gram overlap between generated and reference text. ROUGE is standard for summarization, computing ROUGE-1 (unigrams), ROUGE-2 (bigrams), and ROUGE-L (LCS). BERTScore uses contextual embeddings to meas

steps.input: How do vector embeddings and vector databases work together to enable semantic search in a RAG system?
steps.tool_result:
In a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system, vector embeddings and vector databases work together to enable semantic search by creating a structured representation of documents that allows for efficient similarity searches.

1. Vector Embeddings: Text embeddings are dense vector representations of text data that capture semantic meaning. These embeddings are typically high-dimensional (384–1536 dimensions). Similar texts produce geometrically close vectors in the embedding space. This means that texts with similar meanings will have vectors that are closer together in the vector space.

2. Vector Databases: Vector databases are specialized storage systems optimized for efficient similarity search over these high-dimensional vector embeddings. They store these text embeddings and allow for quick retrieval of documents that are semantically similar to a given query.

In the context of a RAG system, during the indexing phase, documents are split into chunks, converted to dense text embeddings, and stored in the vector database. When a query is made, the system retrieves the most relevant documents from the vector database based on their proximity in the vector space. These retrieved documents are then used to augment the language model's response, providing it with additional context that can improve its output.

This combination of text embeddings and vector databases enables semantic search in a RAG system, allowing it to retrieve relevant documents based on their semantic meaning rather than just matching keywords.

In the context of a RAG system, during the indexing phase, documents are split into chunks, converted to dense text embeddings, and stored in the vector database.
